quick_facts.label: Role and service
quick_facts.value: Assistant Commandant is an officer rank in Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), which is one of the Central Armed Police Forces under the Ministry of Home Affairs. The SSB Rules list Assistant Commandant within the officer ranks.

quick_facts.label: Primary entry route
quick_facts.value: The principal open competitive route is the UPSC Central Armed Police Forces (Assistant Commandants) Examination. Candidates state force preferences in the application; allocation is based on merit position and those preferences.

quick_facts.label: 2026 SSB vacancies and examination date
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 CAPF (AC) cycle, UPSC notified 53 tentative SSB vacancies out of 349 total CAPF Assistant Commandant vacancies, and scheduled the written examination for July 19, 2026. UPSC states that vacancy numbers and the examination date are liable to change.

quick_facts.label: Baseline eligibility in the 2026 cycle
quick_facts.value: The 2026 notice required a bachelor’s degree (or an accepted equivalent), age 20–24 as of August 1, 2026, and compliance with prescribed physical and medical standards. Both men and women were eligible; specified age relaxations applied to eligible categories.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: The Ministry of Home Affairs’ 2026 CAPF (AC) examination rules place Assistant Commandant in Level 10, junior scale, of the pay matrix: ₹56,100–₹177,500 basic-pay range. This is pay-matrix context, not take-home pay; admissible allowances and deductions vary by posting and prevailing government rules.

sections.heading: What the role contributes to
paragraphs: SSB is tasked in its area of responsibility with safeguarding assigned borders, promoting a sense of security among border communities, preventing trans-border crime, smuggling and other illegal activity, preventing unauthorised entry or exit, carrying out civic-action programmes, and undertaking other duties assigned by the Central Government. An Assistant Commandant is an officer in this force structure and may be placed in command or other assigned roles supporting these functions.

The work is therefore operational as well as managerial: officers can be responsible for command, discipline, administration, morale, training and welfare of personnel under their assignment. The precise mix depends on the unit, location and orders rather than being fixed for every Assistant Commandant posting.
bullets: Border-security and anti-smuggling work in the assigned area of responsibility.
Leadership, discipline, administration, training and welfare responsibilities when personnel or a task are placed under the officer’s command.
Participation in civic-action and other duties lawfully assigned to the force.

sections.heading: Entering through UPSC CAPF (AC)
paragraphs: For the canonical route, apply to UPSC’s CAPF (AC) examination when the notification is open and include SSB in the force-preference order. In the 2026 notice, allocation was to be made using the final merit position and the preferences submitted with the application; selecting SSB is therefore not a separate SSB written examination under this route.

The 2026 selection sequence was: a two-paper written examination; Physical Standards Test and Physical Efficiency Test for written-qualified candidates; UPSC Interview/Personality Test for candidates qualifying PST/PET; and medical standards testing. Final merit is based on written-examination and interview/personality-test marks, while appointment also requires meeting medical standards.

The 2026 application deadline was March 12, 2026 and the examination was scheduled for July 19, 2026. Those dates, the fee, vacancy numbers, physical standards and eligibility conditions are cycle-specific, so prospective candidates should use the next UPSC notice rather than assume they will recur unchanged.
bullets: Written papers in 2026: General Ability and Intelligence (250 marks), followed by General Studies, Essay and Comprehension (200 marks).
2026 PET benchmarks included a 100-metre race, 800-metre race and long jump for all candidates; shot put applied to men in that notice.
NCC ‘B’ or ‘C’ certification was desirable in the 2026 notice and considered at interview/personality-test stage, not a mandatory baseline qualification.

sections.heading: Capabilities to build
paragraphs: The UPSC selection design indicates the capabilities an aspirant should develop: logical reasoning, numerical ability and data interpretation; scientific and current-affairs awareness; knowledge of Indian polity, economy, history and geography; clear essay writing; English comprehension and communication; and the fitness required to meet PST/PET and medical standards.

For service readiness, the force’s statutory tasks and command provisions make physical resilience, sound judgement, disciplined leadership, communication, ethical conduct, administration and the ability to work with border communities especially relevant. These are role-aligned capabilities, not a substitute for the formal UPSC eligibility and medical requirements.
bullets: Prepare both objective aptitude/general-awareness material and descriptive essay, précis and comprehension work.
Train specifically for the current notification’s test events and standards rather than relying on informal fitness advice.
Develop leadership and record-keeping habits alongside exam preparation, because force duties include command, discipline, administration, training and welfare.

sections.heading: Posting and working environment
paragraphs: SSB is the border-guarding force assigned to the Indo-Nepal and Indo-Bhutan borders. Its charter also includes preventing cross-border crime and unauthorised movement, as well as civic-action work. Accordingly, an Assistant Commandant can expect assignments connected with border-area units and their operational, administrative or training requirements; the specific station and duties are determined by service needs.

The SSB Rules provide that officers and members of the regular force are liable for continuous service for the term set out in their appointment or applicable rules. They also provide that members may be required to perform duties connected with the force’s task, administration, discipline, training and welfare, as well as other duties assigned under law or by superior order.
bullets: Border-area and unit-based work is central to SSB’s mandate.
Assignments can combine field operations with personnel administration, training and community-facing civic action.
Location, operational tempo and duties vary with the area of responsibility and orders.

sections.heading: Officer hierarchy and early career development
paragraphs: The SSB Rules place Assistant Commandant as an officer rank below Deputy Commandant, Second-in-Command, Commandant, Deputy Inspector-General, Inspector-General, Additional Director-General and Director-General. This establishes the officer hierarchy, but it should not be read as a guarantee of promotion on a particular timetable.

For officers recruited through CAPF (AC), the 2026 Ministry of Home Affairs rules set Assistant Commandant at Level 10 and list the succeeding CAPF scales through Inspector General. The rules also provide for professional training during probation and state that promotion is available only when the eligibility conditions in the relevant recruitment rules are met. Progression therefore depends on applicable rules, training, performance, vacancies and service requirements.
bullets: Entry rank: Assistant Commandant (Level 10 in the 2026 CAPF rules).
Next listed CAPF officer rank: Deputy Commandant (Level 11 in the 2026 rules).
Professional training and any required departmental examinations form part of the probationary development framework.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As notified in the Ministry of Home Affairs’ 2026 CAPF (AC) examination rules, Assistant Commandant is in Pay Matrix Level 10 (junior scale), ₹56,100–₹177,500. The range is the published basic-pay matrix range, not a single monthly in-hand salary.

Actual monthly remuneration can differ by place and nature of posting because allowances, deductions and entitlements are governed by the rules in force and individual service circumstances. Candidates should therefore treat the stated scale as dated 2026 compensation context and verify current government orders before making financial comparisons.
bullets: Do not equate the pay-matrix range with take-home salary.
Allowances and deductions are variable and should be confirmed from current official orders.
